config.title: New York City
config.coverNote: Tyler and Talula, here is your trip hub. Everything is on this page: your flights, your hotel, and the spots I picked for you around Chelsea. Text me if anything needs to move. I will answer.
config.driverPhrase: Can you take me here please?

days.title: Arrival & Mets Game
days.summary: Fly in from LAX, settle in at the Motto Chelsea, Mets vs Dodgers at 7:10pm at Citi Field.

days.title: Full Day in NYC
days.summary: Rebecca's baby shower at noon at Da Andrea, then explore Chelsea Market, the High Line, and the West Village.

days.title: Departure
days.summary: Morning in the city, check out by noon, afternoon flight home.

itinerary.title: Delta 934 to New York
itinerary.subtitle: Delta Comfort | Talula seat 33G, Tyler seat TBC
itinerary.personalNote: Early flight out of LAX. You land at JFK at 2:33pm local time. From JFK, a taxi is a flat $70 plus tip and tolls. Or take the AirTrain to Jamaica station and the LIRR to Penn Station, then a 10-minute subway to the hotel. I would take the taxi. You just flew across the country.

itinerary.title: Motto by Hilton New York City Chelsea
itinerary.personalNote: This is a great location for you. You are in the middle of Chelsea, which means you can walk to 30 great restaurants, the High Line, Chelsea Market, and the West Village. Check-in is at 3pm. If you arrive earlier, they will hold your bags.

itinerary.title: Mets vs Dodgers at Citi Field
itinerary.subtitle: 7:10 PM first pitch
itinerary.personalNote: Mets hosting the Dodgers at 7:10pm. Citi Field is in Queens, about 35 minutes from the hotel on the 7 train from Times Square-42nd St. Take the 1 train from 23rd St to Times Square, transfer to the 7 to Mets-Willets Point. The stadium is right there. Grab a Shake Shack inside the stadium, it is the original recipe.

itinerary.title: Rebecca's Baby Shower at Da Andrea
itinerary.subtitle: Noon
itinerary.personalNote: Rebecca's baby shower at noon at Da Andrea on 8th Ave. This is a 5-minute walk from the hotel, just head north on 8th Ave. Northern Italian, handmade pasta. A lovely spot for it.

itinerary.title: Delta 747 to Los Angeles
itinerary.subtitle: Delta Comfort | Talula seat 31B, Tyler seat TBC
itinerary.personalNote: Your flight is at 2:05pm from JFK. I would leave the hotel by 11:30am to give yourself plenty of time. Check out is at noon. Taxi to JFK takes about 45 minutes on a Sunday afternoon.

picks.name: Los Tacos No. 1
picks.description: The original location of what many consider NYC's best tacos. Adobada on housemade tortillas. Counter-service, no seating. Inside Chelsea Market.
picks.note: Get the adobada taco on a corn tortilla. This is the best taco in New York and it is $4. Go before noon, the line gets long after 1pm.

picks.name: Shukette
picks.description: Levantine/Middle Eastern from the team behind Shuka. The Fish In A Cage and joojeh chicken are must-orders. Bread program is exceptional.
picks.note: Book two weeks out. If you get in, order the Fish In A Cage and the joojeh chicken for the table. The bread alone is worth the reservation.

picks.name: Chelsea Market
picks.description: 55+ food vendors in a converted Nabisco factory where Oreos were invented. The single most worthwhile tourist attraction in NYC.
picks.note: Go at 11am on a weekday, not 1pm on a Saturday. Walk through, graze, and then take the High Line entrance at 16th St upstairs after.

picks.name: The High Line
picks.address: Gansevoort St to W 34th St, New York, NY
picks.description: Elevated park on a former freight rail line. Runs from Meatpacking to Hudson Yards. Free, open daily 7am-8pm.
picks.note: Enter at 16th St right next to Chelsea Market. Walk north to Hudson Yards, or south to the Whitney. Best in the morning or near sunset.

picks.name: L'Industrie Pizzeria
picks.description: Voted best slice in the USA. Long-fermented sourdough. Burrata slice with sea salt and basil. Fig jam and prosciutto slice.
picks.note: Worth the 15-minute walk from the hotel. Get the burrata slice. Expect a line at peak times, go at opening.

picks.name: Cote Korean Steakhouse
picks.description: Michelin-starred Korean steakhouse. The Butcher's Feast is $82 per person and the best luxury value in NYC steak.
picks.note: The Butcher's Feast at $82 per person is the best steak deal in Manhattan. Korean barbecue meets Michelin technique. Book the chef's table if you can.

picks.name: Whitney Museum of American Art
picks.description: Renzo Piano-designed museum. Best American art collection in the city. Hopper, Basquiat, Warhol. Outdoor terraces with Hudson River views.
picks.note: Go Friday evening after 7pm when it is free. Walk the galleries, then out to the terraces for sunset over the Hudson.

picks.name: Faicco's Italian Specialties
picks.description: Open since 1900. The best chicken cutlet sandwich in NYC. Italian deli and grocery.
picks.note: Get the chicken cutlet sandwich. It is the size of a forearm and it is perfect. Grab some jarred pesto on the way out.

picks.name: Joe's Pizza
picks.description: The quintessential New York slice since 1975. No frills, perfect plain slice.
picks.note: Get a plain slice. Fold it. This is the New York slice you have seen in every movie. It is $4 and it is perfect.

picks.name: General Theological Seminary Gardens
picks.description: Hidden gardens open to the public during daylight. A quiet, cloistered space in the middle of Chelsea. Feels like an Oxford quadrangle.
picks.note: Walk through the gate on 9th Ave at 20th St. It is free, it is quiet, and nobody knows it is here. A perfect 10-minute escape.

picks.name: Russ & Daughters
picks.address: 179 E Houston St, New York, NY 10002
picks.description: Since 1914. The full appetizing experience: smoked fish, caviar, babka. The classic bagel with lox.
picks.note: Go to the original shop on Houston, not the cafe. Stand at the counter. Order the classic: everything bagel, scallion cream cheese, Nova lox, tomato, capers. That is how it has been done since 1914.

picks.name: El Quijote
picks.description: Open since 1930. Patti Smith, Bob Dylan, and Andy Warhol were regulars. Classic Spanish, luxe paella, Basque cheesecake.
picks.note: This is in your hotel building. Walk downstairs. Order the paella and the Basque cheesecake. You are eating where Warhol ate.

contacts.name: Gabe
contacts.note: Text me first. Anything moves, I move it.

guideBlocks.heading: Getting around
guideBlocks.body: Everything in this guide is within a 25-minute walk of your hotel. Chelsea, the West Village, the Meatpacking District, and Flatiron are all walkable. For farther neighborhoods, the subway is $2.90 per ride with a phone tap. The 1/2/3 lines run along 7th Ave, two blocks from the hotel.

guideBlocks.heading: From JFK to your hotel
guideBlocks.body: A taxi from JFK is a flat $70 plus tip and tolls, about 45 minutes. Or take the AirTrain to Jamaica station, then the LIRR to Penn Station, then a 10-minute subway ride. I would take the taxi. You just flew across the country.

guideBlocks.heading: Chelsea Market tip
guideBlocks.body: Go at 11am on a weekday, not 1pm on a Saturday. It gets packed. Walk through, graze at Los Tacos No. 1 and Very Fresh Noodles, then take the High Line entrance at 16th St upstairs after.

guideBlocks.heading: The best slice
guideBlocks.body: L'Industrie on Christopher St is the best pizza in America right now. It is a 15-minute walk from the hotel. Get the burrata slice. Go at opening to avoid the line.

essentials.label: Emergency
essentials.note: Dial 911 for police, fire, or medical emergencies anywhere in NYC.

essentials.label: Your hotel, for a taxi driver
